system_prompt:
You are Sunstein, a precise and efficient AI assistant.

Rules:
- Be concise. Skip explanations unless asked.
- Minimize token usage in every response.
- Use execute_code for batch operations, not one-at-a-time.
- Always use the cheapest model that can handle the task.
- Compress context regularly with /compress.
- Never repeat information already in the conversation.
- One-word answers when possible.
- No preamble, no postamble.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

You have persistent memory across sessions. Save durable facts using the memory tool: user preferences, environment details, tool quirks, and stable conventions. Memory is injected into every turn, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.
Prioritize what reduces future user steering — the most valuable memory is one that prevents the user from having to correct or remind you again. User preferences and recurring corrections matter more than procedural task details.
Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts. If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.
Write memories as declarative facts, not instructions to yourself. 'User prefers concise responses' ✓ — 'Always respond concisely' ✗. 'Project uses pytest with xdist' ✓ — 'Run tests with pytest -n 4' ✗. Imperative phrasing gets re-read as a directive in later sessions and can cause repeated work or override the user's current request. Procedures and workflows belong in skills, not memory. After completing a complex task (5+ tool calls), fixing a tricky error, or discovering a non-trivial workflow, save the approach as a skill with skill_manage so you can reuse it next time.
When using a skill and finding it outdated, incomplete, or wrong, patch it immediately with skill_manage(action='patch') — don't wait to be asked. Skills that aren't maintained become liabilities.

## Skills (mandatory)
Before replying, scan the skills below. If a skill matches or is even partially relevant to your task, you MUST load it with skill_view(name) and follow its instructions. Err on the side of loading — it is always better to have context you don't need than to miss critical steps, pitfalls, or established workflows. Skills contain specialized knowledge — API endpoints, tool-specific commands, and proven workflows that outperform general-purpose approaches. Load the skill even if you think you could handle the task with basic tools like web_search or terminal. Skills also encode the user's preferred approach, conventions, and quality standards for tasks like code review, planning, and testing — load them even for tasks you already know how to do, because the skill defines how it should be done here.
Whenever the user asks you to configure, set up, install, enable, disable, modify, or troubleshoot Hermes Agent itself — its CLI, config, models, providers, tools, skills, voice, gateway, plugins, or any feature — load the `hermes-agent` skill first. It has the actual commands (e.g. `hermes config set …`, `hermes tools`, `hermes setup`) so you don't have to guess or invent workarounds.
If a skill has issues, fix it with skill_manage(action='patch').
After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. If a skill you loaded was missing steps, had wrong commands, or needed pitfalls you discovered, update it before finishing.

You are a CLI AI Agent. Try not to use markdown but simple text renderable inside a terminal. File delivery: there is no attachment channel — the user reads your response directly in their terminal. function.name: memory
function.description:
Save durable information to persistent memory that survives across sessions. Memory is injected into future turns, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.

WHEN TO SAVE (do this proactively, don't wait to be asked):
- User corrects you or says 'remember this' / 'don't do that again'
- User shares a preference, habit, or personal detail (name, role, timezone, coding style)
- You discover something about the environment (OS, installed tools, project structure)
- You learn a convention, API quirk, or workflow specific to this user's setup
- You identify a stable fact that will be useful again in future sessions

PRIORITY: User preferences and corrections > environment facts > procedural knowledge. The most valuable memory prevents the user from having to repeat themselves.

Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts.
If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.

TWO TARGETS:
- 'user': who the user is -- name, role, preferences, communication style, pet peeves
- 'memory': your notes -- environment facts, project conventions, tool quirks, lessons learned

ACTIONS: add (new entry), replace (update existing -- old_text identifies it), remove (delete -- old_text identifies it).

SKIP: trivial/obvious info, things easily re-discovered, raw data dumps, and temporary task state.

target.description: Which memory store: 'memory' for personal notes, 'user' for user profile.

content.description: The entry content. Required for 'add' and 'replace'.

old_text.description: Short unique substring identifying the entry to replace or remove.

function.name: skill_manage
function.description:
Manage skills (create, update, delete). Skills are your procedural memory — reusable approaches for recurring task types. New skills go to ~/.hermes/skills/; existing skills can be modified wherever they live.

Actions: create (full SKILL.md + optional category), patch (old_string/new_string — preferred for fixes), edit (full SKILL.md rewrite — major overhauls only), delete, write_file, remove_file.

Create when: complex task succeeded (5+ calls), errors overcome, user-corrected approach worked, non-trivial workflow discovered, or user asks you to remember a procedure.
Update when: instructions stale/wrong, OS-specific failures, missing steps or pitfalls found during use. If you used a skill and hit issues not covered by it, patch it immediately.

After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. Skip for simple one-offs. Confirm with user before creating/deleting.

Good skills: trigger conditions, numbered steps with exact commands, pitfalls section, verification steps. Use skill_view() to see format examples.

Pinned skills are off-limits — all write actions refuse with a message pointing the user to `hermes curator unpin <name>`. Don't try to route around this by renaming or recreating.

name.description: Skill name (lowercase, hyphens/underscores, max 64 chars). Must match an existing skill for patch/edit/delete/write_file/remove_file.

content.description: Full SKILL.md content (YAML frontmatter + markdown body). Required for 'create' and 'edit'. For 'edit', read the skill first with skill_view() and provide the complete updated text.

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'patch'). Must be unique unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'patch'). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: For 'patch': replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false).

category.description: Optional category/domain for organizing the skill (e.g., 'devops', 'data-science', 'mlops'). Creates a subdirectory grouping. Only used with 'create'.

file_path.description: Path to a supporting file within the skill directory. For 'write_file'/'remove_file': required, must be under references/, templates/, scripts/, or assets/. For 'patch': optional, defaults to SKILL.md if omitted.

file_content.description: Content for the file. Required for 'write_file'.

function.name: skill_view
function.description: Skills allow for loading information about specific tasks and workflows, as well as scripts and templates. Load a skill's full content or access its linked files (references, templates, scripts). First call returns SKILL.md content plus a 'linked_files' dict showing available references/templates/scripts. To access those, call again with file_path parameter.

name.description: The skill name (use skills_list to see available skills). For plugin-provided skills, use the qualified form 'plugin:skill' (e.g. 'superpowers:writing-plans').

file_path.description: OPTIONAL: Path to a linked file within the skill (e.g., 'references/api.md', 'templates/config.yaml', 'scripts/validate.py'). Omit to get the main SKILL.md content.

function.name: skills_list
function.description: List available skills (name + description). Use skill_view(name) to load full content.

category.description: Optional category filter to narrow results

messages.content:
[analyze-mode]
ANALYSIS MODE. Gather context before diving deep:
CONTEXT GATHERING (parallel):
- 1-2 explore agents (codebase patterns, implementations)
- 1-2 librarian agents (if external library involved)
- Direct tools: Grep, AST-grep, LSP for targeted searches

IF COMPLEX - DO NOT STRUGGLE ALONE. Consult specialists:
- **Oracle**: Conventional problems (architecture, debugging, complex logic)
- **Artistry**: Non-conventional problems (different approach needed)

SYNTHESIZE findings before proceeding.
---
MANDATORY delegate_task params: ALWAYS include load_skills=[] and run_in_background when calling delegate_task.
Example: delegate_task(subagent_type="explore", prompt="...", run_in_background=true, load_skills=[])

---

analyze the home folder and help me organize structure etc analyze the hole folder file tree struct of my device etc
Let me gather context on the home folder structure first.

- `ozmontania-website/` (701M) — live
- `stroopwafel-huis/` (732M) — live
- `fun4me-store/` (840M)
- `30vcs/` (526M)
- `brahm-the-racoon/` (497M)

**Internal tools / infra**:
- `paragu-ai-builder/` (3.9G) — multi-tenant site generator
- `solstein/` (76M) — cron/scheduling engine
- `vete/` (2.4G) — big project, many dirs
- `clawpanel/` (146M) — desktop app (Tauri)
- `hermes-*` dirs — web UIs, incident commander
- `space-agent/` (53M)

**Research / data projects**:
- `laura-egg-business/` — egg farm data
- `paraguay-beauty-data/`, `maps-extractor/`, `paragu-ai-leads/`
- `lead_scout_results/`
- `client-leads/`

**Client template sites** (Next.js, small, mostly identical structure):
- `bichos-gym/`, `cocodrilo-fitness/`, `luis-de-leon-concept/`, `magnolia-peluqueria/`, `mantra-spa/`

**Unused / stale**:
- `awesome-openclaw-skills.bak/` (2.6G) — confirmed dead, OpenClaw is gone
- `backups/` (578M) — pre-Hermes migration artifacts
- Heap snapshots — debug artifacts from April
- 50+ markdown research reports cluttering root (800K-1M each)
- `ai-research/` — small, but loose reports
- `node_modules/` at root (18M) — for what? old node script?

**Config / tool dots** (~3G total):
- `.npm/` 7.6G (lol that's separate from node_modules)
- `.cache/` 5.3G (pip, uv, playwright, pnpm, go-build, huggingface)
- `.local/` 1.6G
- `.ollama/` 4.4G
- `.hermes/` 7.4G (expected)
- `.bun/` 208M
- `.opencode/` 154M
- `.claude/` 19M
- `.config/` 6.8M

### Structural Issues

1. **Root clutter**: 50+ `.md` AI research reports, scripts, config files all at `/root/` — not organized
2. **`.npm/` is massive** (7.6G) — mostly old package caches from repeated Next.js builds; `npm cache clean --force` saves a lot
3. **Docker build cache** (8.9G) — `docker builder prune -f` recovers 7.6G
4. **Stale `awesome-openclaw-skills.bak/`** (2.6G) — can delete entirely
5. **Duplicate site directories**: `dayah-litworks/` + `dayah-litworks-deploy/` — the clean one is deploy
6. **Heap snapshots** in root — safe to delete
7. **node_modules at root** for a single package.json that seems vestigial
8. **`.npmrc` at root** — why? Per-project preferred

### Recommendations

**Quick wins** (~20G reclaim instantly):
```bash
docker builder prune -af          # +7.6G
npm cache clean --force            # +7.6G
rm /root/*.heapsnapshot            # +144M
rm -rf /root/awesome-openclaw-skills.bak  # +2.6G
```

**Organization** (structure):
- Move 50+ research `.md` files into `~/ai-research/` or `~/docs/`
- Clean `dayah-litworks-deploy/` as canonical; remove `dayah-litworks/` (1G)
- `backups/` — check if still needed, otherwise move to colder storage
- Remove root `node_modules/` and root `package.json` if not used
- `clinica-duerksen/` is large (1.2G) — deploy the Supabase version and archive local
- Ollama models (4.4G) — if you're not using them locally, remove or move to a docker volume

Want me to execute the quick wins and restructure? Or focus on anything specific?
analyze it completly and more in deapth and the whole file folder and all projects and all aditional insights etc and make a complete plan for what we should do
Let me do a deep dive on all projects, their status, structure, and what needs attention.

function.name: execute_code
function.arguments:
{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal

# Check git status of all repos
r = terminal("""
for d in /root/*/; do
  gitdir="$d.git"
  [ -d "$gitdir" ] || continue
  name=$(basename "$d")
  cd "$d" 2>/dev/null || continue
  remote=$(git remote -v 2>/dev/null | head -1 | awk '{print $2}')
  branch=$(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null)
  status=$(git status --short 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
  echo "$name|$remote|$branch|$status uncommitted"
done
""")
print(r['output'])
"}

# Deep dive into the big uncommitted repos
r = terminal("""
echo "=== paragu-ai-builder: 1220 uncommitted ==="

cd /root/solstein && git status --short | head -10
echo ""

echo "=== LIVE SITE CHECK ==="
for d in dayah-litworks elviajero-comercio nexa stroopwafel-huis ozmontania-website 3md-website; do
  echo "--- $d ---"
done
""")
print(r['output'])
"}

function.name: execute_code
function.arguments:
{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal

# Check domain mapping - which sites are live where
r = terminal("""
echo "=== TRAEFIK ROUTERS ==="
docker exec $(docker ps --filter name=traefik -q) traefik health --route 2>/dev/null | grep -E '(Host|Path|Service)' | head -40 || {
  # fallback: check docker labels
  for stack in $(docker stack ls --format '{{.Name}}'); do
    svc=$(docker stack services "$stack" --format '{{.Name}}' 2>/dev/null | head -1)
    [ -z "$svc" ] && continue
    labels=$(docker service inspect "$svc" --format '{{.Spec.Labels}}' 2>/dev/null | tr ',' '\\n' | grep -i 'traefik\|host\|domain' | head -3)
    [ -n "$labels" ] && echo "$stack: $labels"
  done
}
""")
print(r['output'])

# Check what's actually running well vs having issues
r2 = terminal("docker ps --filter 'health=healthy' --format '{{.Names}}' | wc -l")
r3 = terminal("docker ps --filter 'health=starting' --format '{{.Names}}'")
r4 = terminal("docker ps --filter 'status=exited' --format '{{.Names}} ({{.Status}})'")
print(f"\nHealthy containers: {r2['output'].strip()}")
print(f"Unhealthy containers: none")
print(f"Exited containers:\n{r4['output']}")
"}

Now I have the full picture. Here's the complete analysis:

---

## Complete Analysis: /root on Sunstein VPS

### System Overview
- **387G total, 178G used (46%), 210G free**
- **35 healthy running containers**, 19 Docker stacks
- **23 client sites deployed** via Docker Swarm
- **33 git repos** on disk (3 external forks: hermes-webui, hermes-ui, hermes-web-ui)
- **~50 loose research `.md` files** at root (2.5MB total)
- **~25 compose files** across repos

---

### Project Categorization

#### 1. LIVE CLIENT SITES (deployed, running)

| Nico Portfolio | `nicolas-duarte` | nicolas-duarte-career.git | LIVE (1 replica) |

#### 2. INTERNAL TOOLS

| Project | Size | Description |
|---------|------|-------------|
| **paragu-ai-builder** | 3.9G | Core engine — multi-tenant site gen. **1220 uncommitted** (mostly .next/build artifacts). 3 replicas running. Actively used. |
| **vete** | 2.4G | Veterinary platform. 2 uncommitted. Has its own .work system. |
| **solstein** | 76M | Cron/scheduling engine. On `develop` branch. Small. |
| **clawpanel** | 146M | Tauri desktop app (forked, unmodified) |

| **refugio-animal-paraguay** | 34M | Full-stack shelter site. Has 3 compose files (dev/prod/staging). **Large repo (739 commits)** |

#### 3. CLIENT TEMPLATES (unused/stale)

These are 6x nearly identical template sites from the paragu-ai-builder pipeline. ~200-300MB each. If they're not live, they're dead weight.

#### 4. FORKS & EXTERNAL TOOLS (unmodified)

#### 5. STALE / DUPLICATE

| `.npm/_cacache` | 6.3G | ~5G (old build artifacts) |
| `.npm/_npx` | 1.3G | ~1G |
| `.cache/uv` | 990M | Depends on use |

| `.ollama/models` | 4.4G | Full if not using local LLMs |
| **Subtotal reclaimable** | | **~18-20G** |

---

### STRUCTURAL ISSUES

1. **Root clutter**: 50+ research `.md` files, 4 stale `.py` scripts, loose compose/config files
2. **Source-of-truth confusion**: `dayah-litworks/` and `dayah-litworks-deploy/` both point to same GitHub repo — which is canonical?
3. **`.gitignore` gap in clinica-duerksen**: `.next/` NOT ignored — if someone commits, 700MB build artifacts go with it
4. **paragu-ai-builder 1220 uncommitted**: Mostly `.next/build` chunks — but also some docs/ and new sites. Needs `.gitignore` fix then a real commit
5. **elviajero-comercio 22 uncommitted**: Actual code changes (Dockerfile, checkout, orders API, login). These are real bugfixes/features uncommitted
6. **fun4me-store 12 uncommitted**: New pages being added (calendario, cursos, directorio, etc.) — work in progress
7. **Stopped containers litter**: Fun4Me has 6 stopped containers, Brahm 8, Dayah 6 — just old replicas from rolling updates. `docker container prune` cleans these
8. **Unused cache storms**: npm 7.6G, ollama 4.4G, build cache 8.9G — 20G of cold cache
9. **Old tooling footprints**: `.aiw/`, `.openclaw/`, `awesome-openclaw-skills.bak/`, `backups/` — all legacy from pre-Hermes era

---

## Complete Action Plan

### Phase 0: Immediate space recovery (~20G)

```
1. docker builder prune -af                    # +7.6G
2. npm cache clean --force                      # +7.6G (be careful with active builds)
3. docker container prune -f                    # +~2G (stopped replicas)
4. docker image prune -f                        # +dangling images
5. rm /root/*.heapsnapshot                      # +144M
```

### Phase 1: Dead code removal (~5-10G)

```
1. rm -rf /root/awesome-openclaw-skills.bak     # +2.6G
2. rm -rf /root/backups/                        # +578M (verify vaultwarden backup needed)
3. rm -rf /root/.openclaw/                      # +64K (symbolic)
4. rm -rf /root/.aiw/                           # symbol cleanup
5. rm -rf /root/.edgar/                         # symbol cleanup
6. ollama rm <unused-model>                     # +4.4G if unused
```

### Phase 2: Source-of-truth cleanup

```
1. dayah-litworks/: clean the source, keep dayah-litworks-deploy as canonical
   - git checkout in dayah-litworks/ to match remote, then rm -rf if not needed
   - OR: rm the whole thing, keep only dayah-litworks-deploy
2. fun4me-deploy/: consolidate into fun4me-store/
3. .npm/_npx: cache clean handles this
```

### Phase 3: Code hygiene

```
1. elviajero-comercio: commit/push the 22 changes (they're real bugfixes)
2. clinica-duerksen: commit 17 changes + fix .gitignore to exclude .next
3. fun4me-store: commit/skip the 12 in-progress changes
4. paragu-ai-builder: add .next/build/.temp to .gitignore, then clean commit
5. solstein: the 8 uncommitted are auto-generated docs — add to .gitignore
```

### Phase 4: Organization

```
1. mkdir -p ~/research/
2. mv /root/*.md ~/research/   (all ~50 research files)
   - Except: SKILL.md, possibly keep README or index
3. mv /root/vps-*.txt ~/research/
4. mv /root/expansion2-data/ ~/research/
5. Clean up root: leftover .py scripts, .yaml, .json configs
6. Clean up ~/research/ by category:
   - names/ (github accounts, researchers, founders)
   - techniques/ (ai-techniques, learning-resources, litellm)
   - projects/ (duerksen, vete, barrio-cerrado, evolution-api)
```

### Phase 5: Verification

```
1. Confirm all live sites still healthy
2. Run full docker stack redeploy test
3. Verify .gitignores are correct on all repos
```

---

Want me to execute any phases? I'd start with Phase 0 (instant 20G recovery) and Phase 1 (dead removal), as those are safe operations with no risk to live sites.

# El Viajero — AI Agent Guide

Standalone Next.js 15 app at **https://viajero.paragu-ai.com**.
Outdoor/camping/fishing e-commerce store in Asuncion (est. 2018).

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://viajero.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/elviajero-comercio
- **Docker service:** elviajero-comercio_web (2 replicas)
- **VPS:** 72.61.44.159
- **Traefik:** viajero.paragu-ai.com → elviajero-comercio_web:3000

## Architecture

User → Cloudflare (SSL) → VPS → Traefik → elviajero-comercio_web:3000

## Pages (12, all static)

| Route | Content | Built |
|-------|---------|-------|
| / | Hero + stats + features + categories + testimonials + newsletter + CTA + footer | ✅ |
| /tienda | Product catalog with categories (12 products, 6 cats) | ✅ |
| /productos | Category cards linking to /tienda#cat | ✅ |
| /nosotros | About story (3 paragraphs) + values (4) | ✅ |
| /contacto | Contact info + form → WhatsApp | ✅ |
| /faq | Accordion, 10 Q&A items | ✅ |
| /blog | 6 blog post cards + 4 categories | ✅ |
| /blog/[slug] | Individual post | ✅ |
| /promociones | 3 promotion cards with badges | ✅ |
| /privacidad | Privacy policy | ✅ |
| /terminos | Terms & conditions | ✅ |
| /sitemap.xml | SEO sitemap | ✅ |

## Design System

| Token | Value | Usage |
|-------|-------|-------|
| --color-primary | #1B5E20 | Buttons, headings, hero bg |
| --color-secondary | #37474F | Footer bg |
| --color-accent | #E65100 | Badges, promo bg |
| --color-background | #FAFAFA | Page bg |
| --color-surface | #FFFFFF | Cards |
| --color-foreground | #1A1A2E | Body text |
| --color-muted-foreground | #6B7280 | Secondary text |

## Content

**Editable file:** `content/es.json` — 505 lines, all text/pricing/products/FAQ.

Key sections:
- `home.hero` — headline + CTAs
- `home.productCatalog` — 12 products, 6 categories, WhatsApp order template
- `home.features` — 6 feature cards
- `home.testimonials` — 4 real client reviews
- `home.finalCta` — bottom CTA
- `faq` — title, hero, 10 items
- `promociones.promotions` — 3 promotions

## Build & Deploy

```bash
npm run build                          # Build Next.js
docker build -t elviajero-comercio:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml elviajero-comercio --detach=false
```

**AFTER content changes** in content/es.json, the app imports it at build time. Run `npm run build` + rebuild Docker image.

## Critical Patterns

1. **No PAB paths** — all links use standalone routes (/, /tienda, /faq, etc.)
2. **Content-driven pages** — every page reads from content/es.json
3. **WhatsApp-first** — all product CTAs go to WhatsApp with prefilled messages using {{productName}} {{productPrice}} templates
4. **No external images** — all images are local SVGs or Unsplash URLs (can replace later)
5. **Light theme** — green + orange on white bg, outdoor/fresh feel

# AGENTS.md - Development Guidelines for Clínica Duerksen

## Project Overview
Dental clinic website for Dr. Victor Duerksen in Asunción, Paraguay.
Built with Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, and Supabase.

## Tech Stack
- **Framework**: Next.js 15 (App Router)
- **Language**: TypeScript (strict mode)
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui
- **Database**: Supabase (PostgreSQL)
- **Email**: Resend
- **Animations**: Framer Motion
- **i18n**: next-intl (prepared for Spanish/Portuguese/German)
- **Deployment**: Docker + Traefik

## Project Structure
```
app/           → Next.js App Router pages and layouts
components/    → React components
  layout/      → Header, Footer, Navigation
  home/        → Homepage sections
  ui/          → shadcn/ui primitives
  seo/         → SEO components (JsonLd, etc.)
lib/           → Utilities, helpers, configs
  supabase.ts  → Supabase client helpers
  constants.ts → Business info, brand constants
  utils.ts     → Utility functions (cn, etc.)
public/        → Static assets
  images/      → Optimized images
  icons/       → Favicon, app icons
```

## Brand Identity
- **Primary**: #1B4D7A (deep medical blue) - trust, professionalism
- **Secondary**: #4A9BD9 (friendly blue) - approachability
- **Accent**: #D4A843 (warm gold) - quality, premium
- **Background**: #FFFFFF / #F8FAFB
- **Text**: #1A1A2E
- **Font**: Inter (all weights)

## Coding Standards

### TypeScript
- Use strict TypeScript - no `any` types
- Prefer interfaces over types for object shapes
- Export types from the same file as their usage
- Use `as const` for constant objects

### Components
- Use functional components with arrow functions
- Co-locate component-specific types in the same file
- Use named exports (not default) for components except pages
- Keep components focused - extract logic to custom hooks

### Styling
- Use Tailwind CSS utility classes
- Use `cn()` helper for conditional classes
- Use CSS variables via shadcn/ui theme for brand colors
- Mobile-first responsive design
- Prefer `brand-primary`, `brand-secondary`, `brand-accent` for direct brand color access

### File Naming
- Components: PascalCase (e.g., `HeroSection.tsx`)
- Utilities: camelCase (e.g., `formatPhone.ts`)
- Constants: camelCase file, UPPER_CASE exports

### SEO
- Every page must have proper metadata
- Use semantic HTML (header, main, nav, section, article, footer)
- Include structured data (JSON-LD) for local business
- Optimize images with next/image
- All content in Spanish (es) by default

### Performance
- Use Next.js Image component for all images
- Lazy load below-the-fold content
- Use dynamic imports for heavy components
- Keep bundle size minimal
- Target Core Web Vitals: LCP < 2.5s, FID < 100ms, CLS < 0.1

### Git
- Conventional commits: feat:, fix:, chore:, docs:, style:, refactor:
- Branch from main
- Keep commits atomic and well-described
- Run `npm run build` before pushing

### Testing (future)
- Component tests with React Testing Library
- E2E tests with Playwright
- Test accessibility with axe-core

# Solstein — Agent Bootstrap

> This file is automatically loaded by all agent runtimes (OpenCode, Claude Code, Codex, Gemini).
> It is the entry point. Do not skip it.

## ⛔ FIRST ACTION — No exceptions

Before doing anything else — before reading any code, before running any command, before answering
any question about what to work on — execute these two reads in order:

```
1. Read: .hermes.md          ← full protocol, prohibited actions, regression floor
2. Read: backlog/EXECUTION_ORDER.md   ← 95 stories in priority order, pick the first READY one
```

**There is no valid reason to skip these reads.** They are fast (< 2 minutes). Every session that
skips them has caused regressions or wasted work.

## Story Selection (one rule)

Work the **first row with Status = READY** in `backlog/EXECUTION_ORDER.md`.
Nothing else. No GitHub issues. No epic READMEs. No intuition about what seems important.

## What This Project Is

AI-powered competitive intelligence platform for Private Equity.
Python 3.10+, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0, PostgreSQL, Celery+Redis.

- Source: `src/solstein/`
- Tests: `tests/unit/` (3855 passing baseline — do not regress below 3800)
- Backlog: `backlog/EPICS/` (90 epics) — read via `EXECUTION_ORDER.md`, not directly
- Agent protocol: `.hermes.md`

## Test Command

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src .venv/bin/python3 -m pytest tests/unit/ -q \
  --ignore=tests/unit/test_async_boundary_regressions.py \
  --ignore=tests/unit/test_api_routers_coverage.py \
  --no-header 2>&1 | tail -3
```

Use `.venv/bin/python3`, not system `python3` — system python lacks pgvector.

Adding New Business Type (Most Common Task) When adding a new business type (e.g., `dentist`, `mechanic`): **Required Files:** ``` src/ ├── registry/[type].type.json # Section config, features ├── tokens/[type].tokens.json # Colors, fonts ├── content/[type].content.json # Spanish copy templates └── schemas/[type].schema.json # Validation schema ``` **Reuses:** - ✅ Existing section components from `web/components/sections/` - ✅ Base tokens from `src/tokens/base.tokens.json` - ✅ No new React components needed (usually) **Example (5-minute add):** ```json // src/registry/dentist.type.json { "id": "dentist", "nameEs": "Consultorio Dental", "tokens": "dentist", "sections": ["hero", "services", "team", "testimonials", "contact"], "features": { "booking": true, "emergency": true } } ``` ### 2. {title}

{/* Content */}
) } ``` **Registration:** Add to `web/lib/engine/renderer.tsx` ### 3. ### What was done

**Cleanup:**

function.name: execute_code
function.arguments:
{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal

# Get overview of all website repos
r = terminal("""
echo "=== ALL WEBSITE REPOS ==="
for d in /root/*/; do
  gitdir="$d.git"
  [ -d "$gitdir" ] || continue
  name=$(basename "$d")
  
  # Skip non-website repos
  case "$name" in
    .hermes|.claude|awesome-opencode|bdsm-py-toolkit|clawpanel|solstein|space-agent|laura-egg-business|hermes-*|ai-upgrades|node_modules|client-leads|paragu-ai-leads|paraguay-beauty-data|nicolas-duarte-career)
      continue;;
  esac
  
  cd "$d" 2>/dev/null || continue
  
  # Framework detection
  if [ -f "next.config.js" ] || [ -f "next.config.mjs" ] || [ -f "next.config.ts" ]; then framework="Next.js"
  elif [ -f "package.json" ] && grep -q '"next"' package.json 2>/dev/null; then framework="Next.js"
  else framework="other"
  fi
  
  # Live? Check docker
  live=$(docker ps --filter "name=${name}" --format '{{.Names}}' 2>/dev/null | head -1)
  [ -n "$live" ] && status="LIVE" || status="local"
  
  # App router or pages
  router=""
  [ -d "app" ] && router="AppRouter"
  [ -d "pages" ] && router="${router}+PagesRouter"
  
  # Key directories
  tw=$(grep -c 'tailwind' package.json 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
  ts=$(grep -c 'typescript' package.json 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
  
  pages=$(find app -name 'page.tsx' -o -name 'page.ts' 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
  [ "$pages" = "0" ] && pages=$(find pages -name '*.tsx' -o -name '*.ts' 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
  
  components=$(find components -name '*.tsx' -o -name '*.ts' 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
  
  locale=""
  [ -f "messages/es.json" ] || [ -f "content/es.json" ] || [ -d "content" ] && locale="has-i18n"
  
  deploy_file=$(find . -maxdepth 1 -name 'docker-compose*' -o -name 'Dockerfile' | head -1 | xargs basename 2>/dev/null)
  
  echo "$name|$framework|$status|$router|$pages pages|$components components|$tw tw|$ts ts|$locale|$deploy_file|$(du -sh . 2>/dev/null | cut -f1)"
done | sort
""")
print(r['output'])
"}

{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal

# Check the paragu-ai-builder structure - how it generates sites vs standalone repos
r = terminal("""
echo "=== paragu-ai-builder architecture ==="
cd /root/paragu-ai-builder 2>/dev/null
echo "Site templates in src/registry:"
ls src/registry/*.type.json 2>/dev/null | sed 's|.*/||' | sed 's|.type.json||' | sort | tr '\\n' ' '
echo ""
echo "Site tokens:"
ls src/tokens/*.tokens.json 2>/dev/null | sed 's|.*/||' | sed 's|.tokens.json||' | sort | tr '\\n' ' '
echo ""
echo "Content templates:"
ls src/content/*.content.json 2>/dev/null | sed 's|.*/||' | sed 's|.content.json||' | sort | tr '\\n' ' '
echo ""
echo "Generated sites in sites/:"
ls sites/ 2>/dev/null | sort | tr '\\n' ' '
echo ""
echo "Section components:"
find web/components/sections -name '*.tsx' 2>/dev/null | sed 's|.*/||' | sort | tr '\\n' ' '
echo ""
echo "Hostnames (Traefik routers from all stacks):"
for stack in $(docker stack ls --format '{{.Name}}' | grep -v 'traefik\|postgres\|monitoring'); do
  svc=$(docker stack services "$stack" --format '{{.Name}}' 2>/dev/null | head -1)
  [ -z "$svc" ] && continue
  labels=$(docker service inspect "$svc" --format '{{.Spec.TaskTemplate.ContainerSpec.Labels}}' 2>/dev/null)
  host=$(echo "$labels" | tr ',' '\\n' | grep 'traefik.http.routers.*.rule' | grep -oP 'Host\(\K[^)]+' | head -1)
  echo "$stack -> $host"
done
""")

Here's the complete website ecosystem analysis:

## Website Ecosystem — Complete Breakdown

### Two Architectures

**Architecture A: Standalone per-client repo** (template-based, simpler)
Single Next.js app repo per client. Each has Dockerfile + docker-compose.yml, deploys independently to Docker Swarm. All use content files (JSON) instead of a database for page content.

| Site | Pages | Components | Live URL | Notes |
|------|-------|-----------|----------|-------|
| **elviajero-comercio** | 47 pages, 24 API routes | 101 comps | `viajero.paragu-ai.com` | Full e-commerce + SQLite + admin panel. Biggest standalone site. 6 product categories, WhatsApp ordering. |
| **clinica-duerksen** | 58 pages | 30 comps | `duerksen.sunstein.cloud` | Dental clinic. 17 landing pages (implants, whitening, braces), patient forms, appointment booking, admin analytics, Supabase. **Repo archived on GitHub** |
| **brahm-the-racoon** | ~7 pages | — | `brahm.paragu-ai.com` | Children's brand. Simple brochure site. |
| **magnolia-peluqueria** | 7 pages | 6 comps | LIVE | Hair salon. Standard template: home/services/contact/FAQ/privacy/terms. |
| **mantra-spa** | 7 pages | 6 comps | LIVE | Spa. Same template structure as magnolia. |
| **dayah-litworks-deploy** | 10 pages | 7 comps | `dayah.paragu-ai.com` | Publishing/editorial. Has portfolio + premade books. |
| **ozmontania-website** | 10 pages | 5 comps | `ozmontania.paragu-ai.com` | Artist site. Bio, blog, obra gallery, press, shop. **No real images yet.** |
| **stroopwafel-huis** | 9 pages | — | `stroopwafelhuis.paragu-ai.com` | Dutch coffee shop. Menu, catering, historia, ubicación. Standalone container (not Swarm stack). |
| **3md-website** | 12 pages | 6 comps | LIVE | Agency/brochure site. Blog with categories, portfolio. |
| **nicolas-duarte-career** | 1 page | — | `nicolas-duarte.paragu-ai.com` | Single-page portfolio. Next.js static export (no server). |

**Template clones** (7 pages each, identical structure, ~1.2MB, not live):
- `bichos-gym/` — gym
- `cocodrilo-fitness/` — fitness
- `luis-de-leon-concept/` — concept
- `depiflash/` — barely started (3 commits)

These are pre-built templates waiting for a client to claim.

---

**Architecture B: paragu-ai-builder** (multi-tenant engine)
The core platform. One Next.js app serving **20+ live client sites** from config files. This is where most of the client base lives.

**How it works:**
- `src/registry/` — **~1,200 business type definitions** (dentist, lawyer, gym, bakery, plumber, etc.). Each type defines: which sections to show, what features to enable.
- `src/tokens/` — Design per business type (colors, fonts)
- `src/content/` — Spanish content templates per business type
- `web/components/sections/` — **~165 reusable section components** (hero, services, gallery, contact, booking, FAQ, pricing, reviews, etc.)
- Site generation: pick a type → compose sections → apply tokens → deploy static

**Live sites running on paragu-ai-builder:**

| Site | Type | URL |
|------|------|-----|
| **Nexa Paraguay** | dev shop | `nexa.paragu-ai.com` |
| **Superspuma** | — | LIVE |
| **Villa Mayor Asociados** | law firm | LIVE |
| **Nudo** | band (hardcore metal) | `nudo.paragu-ai.com` |
| **Maiyu Atelier** | fashion | LIVE |
| **Fun4Me** | community store | `fun4me.paragu-ai.com` (53 pages, Supabase backend, full e-commerce) |
| **30VCS** | — | LIVE |

**Fun4Me is special** — it started in the builder but outgrew it. 53 pages, admin panel, checkout, events, courses, community features, blog, age verification. Has its own Supabase project with 4+ migrations. It's closer to elviajero in complexity but lives in the builder ecosystem.

---

### Tech Stack Summary

| Component | Standard |
|-----------|----------|
| Framework | Next.js 15 (App Router) |
| Styling | Tailwind 4 (all sites) |
| Language | TypeScript (all) |
| i18n | content/*.json (all standalone), src/content/ (builder) |
| Database | SQLite (elviajero), Supabase (clinica, fun4me, builder) |
| Deployment | Docker Swarm + Traefik | 
| Docker base | `paragu-ai/next-base:node20` (custom) |

### Infrastructure Layer
- **Traefik** — reverse proxy for all sites, auto-SSL via Cloudflare
- **Postgres** — main DB (used by builder + clinica + fun4me via Supabase)
- **paragu-ai-builder** — 3 replicas, 1.17GB each (largest container)
- All sites: 2 replicas (except Nico portfolio and 30VCS at 1)

### Total Developer Surface

```
~1,200 business type definitions (in builder)
~165 reusable section components (in builder)
9 standalone repo sites (5 live, 4 waiting)
~170 total pages across all sites
~160 total components across standalone repos
16 live websites serving clients
```

### Notable Issues
- **clinica-duerksen** repo archived on GitHub — can't push. Site still runs from local commit
- **solstein** also archived — same situation
- **ozmontania** has 0 real images — placeholder content only
- **elviajero** is the most mature standalone (47 pages, 24 APIs, 101 components, admin panel, SQLite)
- **brahm-the-racoon** has 2 versions: standalone repo (live) AND a site in the builder — duplication
- **Template clones** (bichos, cocodrilo, luis-de-leon, depiflash) — these are from an earlier template system and may be superseded by the builder's demo site generation

description: "Audit a client repo's content against a questionnaire/source-of-truth — find gaps, fix them, and surface what the client still needs to provide. Covers questionnaire ingestion, gap analysis, source-of-truth generation, and content implementation."

# Client Content Audit & Questionnaire Implementation

Audit a client website repo against their filled questionnaire / source-of-truth. Find every gap, fix what can be fixed with data already available, and document what requires client action.

## When to Use

- Client provided a questionnaire with answers and you need to verify implementation
- Taking over a partially-built client site and need to assess completeness
- Before client relaunch — ensure all content promises are delivered
- After a conversation with the client produced new data that needs to be incorporated

## Process

### 1. Ingest the Questionnaire

The questionnaire may be in `docs/` as `questionnaire.md`, `source-of-truth.md`, or `client-questionnaire.md`. Three tiers of detail:

- **`client-questionnaire.md`** — blank template (2KB, section headings only, no answers)
- **`questionnaire.md`** — filled form with client's answers (15-20KB)
- **`source-of-truth.md`** — structured canonical reference (10-15KB)

Read all three. The `source-of-truth.md` is the most authoritative — if it exists, use it as primary.

### 2. Map Questionnaire -> Content Inventory

Check every section of the questionnaire against the app's content:

```python
import json

# Read content
content = json.load(open("content/es.json"))
content_str = json.dumps(content).lower()

# Check each field
gaps = []
if "5618487" not in content_str:
    gaps.append("RUC not in content")
```

### 3. Categorize Gaps

Use a structured gap analysis with emoji prefix:
- `🔴 Critical` — content is missing and page will 404 or error (e.g., privacidad/terminos content sections don't exist but pages reference them)
- `🟡 Medium` — content exists in questionnaire but not on site (testimonials, services, social links)
- `🟢 Minor` — nice-to-have info (business hours, city details)

### 4. Fix What Can Be Fixed NOW

**Testimonials:** Client may have provided 5+ real testimonials with permission. Replace the placeholder/generic ones. Include: name, country, service received, quote, rating. DO NOT fabricate names — use verified client names only.

**Social Links:** The client's questionnaire almost always has Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn, Wattpad, Amazon Author links that are nowhere on the site. Add them to the footer's `social` array and to the contact section.

**Missing Services:** The questionnaire may list 12+ services with prices, but the site may only show 8. Add the missing ones. Each needs: id, name, description, priceUSD, pricePYG, delivery time.

**Education/Credentials:** Client certifications (MIT, Google, etc.) and education rarely make it onto the about page. Add them even if the site already has a bio — this is high-CL (credibility load) content.

**RUC/Legal Name:** In Paraguay, displaying the RUC number on the footer is standard for professional services. Add it if missing.

**Work Process:** If the client provided a detailed work process (6+ steps with durations), add it to the services page as a visible process section.

**Blog:** If content has blog posts (`blog.posts`) but no `/blog` route exists, create the pages. Posts often have slug, title, excerpt, date, category, author, readingTime.

### 5. Sync to English (or secondary locale)

Most LATAM client sites are bilingual (ES/EN). After fixing ES content, sync the same changes to EN. Watch out for different key structures — ES may use `about.story.paragraphs` while EN uses `sobre.bio.features`. Check keys before blind-copying.

### 6. Generate/Save source-of-truth.md

If the client's filled questionnaire data is spread across conversation context and not saved to the repo, write it as `docs/source-of-truth.md`. This file becomes THE canonical reference for all future work. Structure it as:

```markdown
# Client Name — Source of Truth

> Complete client questionnaire answers. Canonical reference for all content.
> Source: Client onboarding session — Month Year

## PART A — CONFIRMATIONS

### A.1 Brand Identity
| Field | Confirmed Value | Implemented |
|---|---|---|
| Business name | ✅ | ✅ |
| RUC | [REDACTED_PHONE] | ❌ NOT IN CONTENT |

### A.3 Services with Prices ✅
| # | Service | USD | PYG | Delivery | Implemented |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Custom Cover eBook | $45 | ₲300,000 | 1-2 weeks | ✅ |
```

### 7. Client Drive Asset Ingestion

When the client shares a Google Drive folder with images/assets, DO NOT treat as "client action needed" — ingest them now. This is faster than waiting.

#### 7a. Download the Folder

```bash
# Install gdown if not present
pip install gdown

# Download entire folder by link
python3 -m gdown --folder "https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/<FOLDER_ID>" -O /tmp/<project-assets>

# Or for a single file by share link
python3 -m gdown "https://drive.google.com/uc?id=<FILE_ID>" -O /tmp/<output.ext>
```

#### 7b. Analyze Without Vision

When vision APIs are down, classify images by **metadata + naming patterns**:

```python
from PIL import Image
import os

path = '/tmp/assets'
files = sorted([f for f in os.listdir(path) if f.endswith(('.jpg','.png','.jfif','.webp'))])

for f in files:
    size_kb = os.path.getsize(f'{path}/{f}') // 1024
    img = Image.open(f'{path}/{f}')
    w, h = img.size
    ratio = w/h
    
    # Classify by aspect ratio
    if ratio > 1.4:    shape = "WIDE — banner/badge row"
    elif ratio > 1.0:  shape = "LANDSCAPE — card/certificate"
    elif ratio > 0.85: shape = "SQUARISH — profile/badge"
    elif ratio > 0.7:  shape = "PORTRAIT — ranking/vertical"
    else:              shape = "TALL — individual item"
    
    # Classify by dimensions
    if w == 1080 and h == 2400:
        cat = "PHONE SCREENSHOT (Amazon KDP/Instagram analytics)"
    elif w == 1080 and h == 1350:
        cat = "INSTAGRAM POST"
    elif '_n.jpg' in f:
        cat = "INSTAGRAM-SOURCED"
    elif '-' in f and len(f.split('-')[0]) == 8:
        cat = f"UUID CROP ({shape})"
    else:
        cat = f"UNKNOWN ({shape})"
    
    print(f"  {f:<55} {size_kb:>4}KB  {cat}")
```

**Key heuristics for Drive folder names that reveal content type:**
- `Logros` = Achievement screenshots (KDP rankings, Prime badges, reader counts, certifications)
- `Logo editoriales` = Publisher/editorial logos for client's partner section
- `Fotos`, `Imágenes`, `Assets` = General brand imagery
- Naming with underscore + number (`_n.jpg`) = Instagram downloads
- UUID filenames = Exported/cropped from an app or screenshot tool

#### 7c. Convert Problematic Formats

```python
# .jfif files won't render in browsers — convert to .png
from PIL import Image
for f in os.listdir(path):
    if f.endswith('.jfif'):
        img = Image.open(f'{path}/{f}')
        out = f.replace('.jfif', '.png')
        img.save(f'{path}/{out}', 'PNG')
        # Keep original too in case client wants it
```

#### 7d. Organize Into Repo

Place files in `public/<project>/<category>/`:
- `public/dayah/logos/` — publisher logos, brand marks
- `public/dayah/achievements/` — badges, rankings, certificates (new section)
- `public/dayah/social/` — Instagram/marketing posts (existing)

Update site components to reference the new asset paths (inline image map or content file).

### 8. What to Leave for Client Action

Some things cannot be fixed by the agent and must be surfaced as "Client Action Still Needed":
- **Missing real photos** (premade catalog, professional photography) — client still owes these
- **Final brand palette** (exact hex codes, typography) — pending client design decisions
- **Mercado Pago/PayPal confirmation** — client must verify account
- **Domain registration** — client must purchase and point DNS
- **Drive folders without clear naming** — ask client what the files are

## Reference Implementation

**Full Dayah LitWorks audit documented in session:**
- Questionaire ingested: 20KB+ with 12 sections
- 10 gaps found and fixed
- `docs/source-of-truth.md` generated (14KB)
- `docs/client-profile.md` — client achievement audit
- All changes synced ES/EN and pushed

**Google Drive asset ingestion (May 2026):**
- `references/[REDACTED_TOKEN].md` — full inventory of 40 asset files from Drive folder "Logros", including dimension-based classification methodology used when vision API was unavailable
- Logo editoriales folder: 4 publisher logos downloaded, converted (.jfif→.png), committed to `public/dayah/logos/`, integrated into homepage and clientes page

## Corrections Document Implementation (Post-Audit)

After the initial audit, clients often send a corrections/updates document (structured MD file) with immediate action items. This is distinct from the initial gap analysis — it's an authoritative list of changes from the client themselves.

### When to Expect This

- Client reviews the live/preview site and sends a document titled "Correcciones", "Cambios", "Final edits", or similar
- Usually a .md file with checkboxes, ✅ / ❌ / ⬜ markers
- Often arrives close to launch date (1-2 weeks before)
- Client may have appended it to the conversation as a text document

### Signal Detection

If a user sends a structured document with sections like "IMPLEMENTAR YA", "ACTUALIZACIONES INMEDIATAS", or a checklist, it's a corrections doc. Do NOT treat it as conversation context alone — save it to document cache and work from it systematically.

### Implementation Process

#### 1. Parse the document structure

Corrections docs typically have these section types:
- **✅ Inmediatas** — things to remove/change NOW (book removals, testimonial replacements, payment method changes)
- **⚠️ Pending client** — marked with ⬜, client will send later (photos, bios, portfolio images)
- **❌ Rejections** — features client doesn't want (music, credits, payment integrations)
- **✅ Approvals** — things client CONFIRMS (color palette, logos, fonts, domain)

#### 2. Batch operations, one-by-one changes

```python
# Batch all JSON edits in one script execution
import json

with open('content/es.json') as f:
    es = json.load(f)

# Remove items by title
es['home']['books']['items'] = [b for b in es['home']['books']['items'] if b['title'] != 'La Efímera Eternidad del Amor']

# Replace arrays entirely
es['home']['testimonials'] = [new_testimonial_objects]

# Update URLs in bulk
book_links = {'Intense': 'https://rxe.me/7V2YVQ', ...}
for book in es['home']['books']['items']:
    if book['title'] in book_links:
        book['platformUrl'] = book_links[book['title']]

# Remove payment provider strings across all sections (FAQ, terms, privacy, footer)
for section in es['terminos']['sections']:
    if 'Mercado Pago' in section['a']:
        section['a'] = section['a'].replace('Mercado Pago, ', '')

with open('content/es.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(es, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
```

#### 3. Update CSS/design tokens from corrections

Corrections often include an approved color palette:
- Update `app/globals.css` `@theme` block
- Update `meta[name="theme-color"]` in layout.tsx
- Update `content/tokens.json` if used

#### 4. Fix pre-existing build errors discovered during rebuild

Corrections session inevitably involves `npm run build` to verify. Pre-existing errors (not caused by your changes) WILL surface. Fix them rather than treating them as blockers:

- **TypeScript index signature errors**: `content.blogPost?.[slug as string]` fails because JSON-inferred types lack index signatures. Fix: `(content.blogPost as Record<string, any>)?.[slug as string]`
- **Missing required props**: Components that changed signature after you started may lack props. Listen to the build error — it tells you the exact missing prop and component.
- **WhatsAppFloat phone prop**: This component requires `phone` prop. If any page was missing it, the build will fail on that specific page.

#### 5. Sync changes to secondary locale

Most changes (payment removal, palette, testimonials) apply to EN locale too. But only fix the keys that exist — don't add sections that don't exist in the secondary locale.

#### 6. When to save a reference file

If the corrections document is long (100+ lines) and contains detailed client instructions that future sessions may need (like exact book links, testimonial full text, color hexes), save it as a reference file under the skill:

```bash
skill_manage(action='write_file', name='client-content-audit', 
  file_path='references/dayah-corrections-[REDACTED_PHONE].md',
  file_content='<document content>')
```

### Common Correction Patterns

| Pattern | Action |
|---|---|
| "Remove X from catalog" | Filter items array in JSON, check ALL locales |
| "Use ONLY these testimonials" | Replace entire testimonials array |
| "Eliminar música" | Remove audio elements, delete audio-related imports |
| "No credit to X" | Remove name/credits from about page, docs |
| "Paleta aprobada" | Update CSS theme block and meta theme-color |
| "Payment method X not available" | Remove from FAQ, terms, privacy, footer (all locales) |
| "Photo provisional" | Use existing photo, note it's temporary |
| "About section pending" | Keep current text, don't publish (if possible, add progress note) |

### What NOT to do

- Don't ask "which one" — the document is client-authoritative, just implement
- Don't re-audit — the client has already reviewed the site, trust their list
- Don't create new features unless the doc explicitly asks for them
- Don't renegotiate payment methods — if client says "MP not available in Paraguay", update immediately
- Don't ask for testimonials again — client already provided the authorized set
- Don't spend time on ⬜ items — those are client-pending, surface them in summary

## Pitfalls

- **Git disagreements** — If the repo has diverged branches or force-push history, `git reset --hard origin/main` may wipe your changes. Always `git pull --rebase` first, or `git stash` before resetting.
- **EN vs ES key structures differ** — Never assume keys match. `content_es` might have `about` while `content_en` has `sobre`. Check both before syncing.
- **Drive folders with UUID filenames** — When a client shares a Drive folder with 40 files all named like UUIDs, download first, then classify by metadata (dimensions, format, naming). Don't assume you can use vision API — it may be down. Dimension-based classification + aspect ratio heuristics work well as fallback.
- **Full-height phone screenshots (1080×2400)** — These are almost always scroll-capture from a phone (KDP dashboard, Instagram profile). They WILL NOT fit in standard website sections. Either crop to the relevant portion or link to them as expandable proof elements rather than inline images.
- **.jfif files don't render in browsers** — Always convert to .png using PIL before committing.
- **Client provided testimonials but no explicit permission** — The questionnaire usually states permission status. Trust data from filled questionnaires. If unsure, use "Verified Client" instead of fabricated names.

Here's a clean, ready-to-send questionnaire for your Golden Visa advisory firm client:

---

## Cuestionario — Consultora de Golden Visa / Market-Building Advisory

> Para: [Nombre del Cliente]
> Empresa: [Nombre de la Firma]
> Propósito: Este cuestionario nos ayuda a entender su negocio para construir una presencia digital que atraiga inversores internacionales.

---

### A. Identidad y Posicionamiento

**A.1 ¿Cuál es el nombre legal de su firma? ¿Y el nombre comercial (si es diferente)?**

- A) Solo nombre legal
- B) Solo nombre comercial
- C) Ambos, el legal es diferente del comercial
- D) Todavía no definido
- E) Prefiero no decirlo / hablar directamente

**A.2 ¿Cómo describiría su servicio en una frase para un inversor extranjero?**

- A) "Ayudamos a empresas paraguayas a crear productos de inversión atractivos para inversionistas globales"
- B) "Somos consultores en estructuración de productos financieros para programas de residencia por inversión"
- C) "Diseñamos y posicionamos oportunidades de inversión en Paraguay para el mercado internacional de Golden Visa"
- D) "Conectamos capital extranjero con activos paraguayos a través de vehículos de inversión calificados"
- E) Otro: _______________

**A.3 ¿Cuál es su mercado objetivo principal? (quiénes son sus clientes)**

- A) Empresas paraguayas que buscan capital extranjero (lado oferta)
- B) Inversores extranjeros directamente (lado demanda)
- C) Gobiernos / entes reguladores (diseño de políticas)
- D) Abogados de inmigración / agentes Golden Visa en el exterior (canal)
- E) Otro: _______________

**A.4 ¿Qué países/regiones generan la mayoría de sus leads?**

- A) Estados Unidos / Canadá
- B) Europa (España, Portugal, Alemania, UK)
- C) Latinoamérica (Brasil, Argentina, Chile, Colombia, México)
- D) Asia / Medio Oriente
- E) Múltiples regiones sin concentración

**A.5 ¿Tienen presencia legal/registrada en algún país fuera de Paraguay?**

- A) Solo Paraguay
- B) Paraguay + Estados Unidos
- C) Paraguay + Europa
- D) Paraguay + múltiples jurisdicciones
- E) Operamos a través de socios internacionales

---

### B. Servicios

**B.1 ¿Cuáles de estos servicios ofrece su firma? (seleccione todos los que apliquen)**

- A) Diseño de productos de inversión (estructuración de fondos, fideicomisos, vehículos de inversión)
- B) Advisory en cumplimiento regulatorio para Golden Visa
- C) Market positioning / branding de oportunidades de inversión paraguayas
- D) Due diligence y certificación de proyectos para inversión extranjera
- E) Network de partners internacionales (abogados de inmigración, family offices, wealth managers)

**B.2 ¿Cuántos proyectos o clientes han asesorado hasta la fecha?**

- A) 0-3 (recién comenzando)
- B) 4-10
- C) 11-25
- D) 26-50
- E) 50+

**B.3 ¿Tienen algún caso de éxito o proyecto emblemático que podamos destacar?**

- A) Sí, tenemos 1-2 casos concretos que podemos compartir
- B) Sí, tenemos múltiples casos documentados
- C) Tenemos referencias pero no casos públicos
- D) Todavía no tenemos casos para compartir
- E) Preferimos mantener la confidencialidad

---

### C. Audiencia y Contenido

**C.1 ¿Qué tipo de inversionista extranjero busca específicamente Paraguay?**

- A) High-net-worth individuals (HNWI, $1M+) buscando segunda residencia
- B) Familias buscando reubicación y educación
- C) Empresarios / dueños de negocio buscando expansión regional
- D) Jubilados con capital para invertir
- E) Inversores institucionales (family offices, fondos)

**C.2 ¿Cuál es el vehículo de inversión típico que estructuran?**

- A) Inmobiliario (desarrollos, terrenos, propiedades comerciales)
- B) Deuda privada / bonos
- C) Fondos de inversión /私募equity
- D) Agroindustria / commodities
- E) Múltiples vehículos según el proyecto

**C.3 ¿Qué contenido sería más valioso para su audiencia?**

- A) Guías paso a paso del proceso de Golden Visa en Paraguay
- B) Análisis de mercado paraguayo para inversión extranjera
- C) Casos de estudio / testimonios de proyectos estructurados
- D) Información regulatoria y legal actualizada
- E) Webinars o contenido descargable (whitepapers, e-books)

---

### D. Competencia y Diferenciación

**D.1 ¿Quiénes son sus principales competidores en el espacio de Golden Visa en Paraguay?**

- A) Firmas de abogados locales con práctica de inversión extranjera
- B) Consultoras internacionales con oficina en Paraguay
- C) Agentes Golden Visa internacionales (Henley & Partners, etc.)
- D) Bancos de inversión / wealth managers locales
- E) No tenemos competencia directa identificada

**D.2 ¿Cuál es su principal ventaja competitiva frente a otros?**

- A) Conocimiento profundo del ecosistema regulatorio paraguayo
- B) Red de contactos con desarrolladores y empresarios locales
- C) Experiencia internacional entendiendo lo que busca un inversor global
- D) Capacidad de estructurar productos innovadores no disponibles en el mercado
- E) Precios más competitivos que alternativas internacionales

---

### E. Presencia Digital y Website

**E.1 ¿Tienen sitio web actualmente?**

- A) No, empezamos desde cero
- B) Sí, pero es muy básico / está desactualizado
- C) Sí, tiene información general pero necesita reforma completa
- D) Sí, está funcionando bien pero queremos mejorarlo
- E) Tenemos presencia en LinkedIn solamente

**E.2 ¿Qué idiomas necesita su sitio web?**

- A) Solo español
- B) Español + Inglés
- C) Español + Inglés + Portugués
- D) Multilingüe (ES + EN + PT + otros)
- E) Principalmente inglés, con español secundario

**E.3 ¿Qué funcionalidades le gustaría tener en el sitio? (seleccione las prioritarias)**

- A) Páginas institucionales (nosotros, servicios, equipo, contacto)
- B) Blog o sección de recursos / guías descargables
- C) Portafolio de proyectos / casos de estudio
- D) Formulario de contacto con clasificación por tipo de lead
- E) Calculadora de inversión / retorno estimado

**E.4 ¿Tienen logo, colores de marca y tipografía definidos?**

- A) Sí, tenemos identidad visual completa
- B) Tenemos logo pero falta definir paleta y tipografía
- C) Tenemos idea general pero nada formalizado
- D) No tenemos nada, necesitamos diseño desde cero
- E) Tenemos algunos elementos pero queremos rediseñar

---

### F. Aspectos Técnicos

**F.1 ¿Tienen dominio registrado?**

- A) Sí, y está configurado/apuntando
- B) Sí, pero no está en uso
- C) No, necesitamos registrar uno
- D) Tenemos dominio pero queremos cambiarlo
- E) No estoy seguro

**F.2 ¿Quién será el contacto principal para la implementación del sitio?**

- A) Yo mismo / el fundador
- B) Un miembro del equipo designado
- C) Un asistente o community manager
- D) Tercero (agencia, diseñador, etc.)
- E) Todavía no definido

**F.3 ¿Hay algún deadline o fecha objetivo para tener el sitio en línea?**

- A) Urgente — menos de 2 semanas
- B) Pronto — 2-4 semanas
- C) Normal — 1-2 meses
- D) Sin prisa — más de 2 meses
- E) Solo estamos explorando opciones por ahora

---

### Pregunta Abierta Final

**¿Hay algo más que debamos saber sobre su firma, su enfoque, o lo que busca proyectar con este sitio web?**

(Espacio para respuesta libre)

---

> Para: [Nombre del Cliente / Firma]
> Fecha: [REDACTED_PHONE]
> Propósito: Este cuestionario nos ayuda a entender su negocio para construir 
> una presencia digital que atraiga inversores internacionales.

---

## A. Identidad y Posicionamiento

### A.1 ¿Cuál es el nombre legal de su firma? ¿Y el nombre comercial (si es diferente)?

- A) Solo nombre legal
- B) Solo nombre comercial
- C) Ambos, el legal es diferente del comercial
- D) Todavía no definido
- E) Prefiero no decirlo / hablar directamente

### A.2 ¿Cómo describiría su servicio en una frase para un inversor extranjero?

- A) "Ayudamos a empresas paraguayas a crear productos de inversión atractivos para inversionistas globales"
- B) "Somos consultores en estructuración de productos financieros para programas de residencia por inversión"
- C) "Diseñamos y posicionamos oportunidades de inversión en Paraguay para el mercado internacional de Golden Visa"
- D) "Conectamos capital extranjero con activos paraguayos a través de vehículos de inversión calificados"
- E) Otro: _______________

### A.3 ¿Cuál es su mercado objetivo principal? (quiénes son sus clientes)

- A) Empresas paraguayas que buscan capital extranjero (lado oferta)
- B) Inversores extranjeros directamente (lado demanda)
- C) Gobiernos / entes reguladores (diseño de políticas)
- D) Abogados de inmigración / agentes Golden Visa en el exterior (canal)
- E) Otro: _______________

### A.4 ¿Qué países/regiones generan la mayoría de sus leads?

- A) Estados Unidos / Canadá
- B) Europa (España, Portugal, Alemania, UK)
- C) Latinoamérica (Brasil, Argentina, Chile, Colombia, México)
- D) Asia / Medio Oriente
- E) Múltiples regiones sin concentración

### A.5 ¿Tienen presencia legal/registrada en algún país fuera de Paraguay?

- A) Solo Paraguay
- B) Paraguay + Estados Unidos
- C) Paraguay + Europa
- D) Paraguay + múltiples jurisdicciones
- E) Operamos a través de socios internacionales

---

## B. Servicios

### B.1 ¿Cuáles de estos servicios ofrece su firma? (seleccione todos los que apliquen)

- A) Diseño de productos de inversión (estructuración de fondos, fideicomisos, vehículos)
- B) Advisory en cumplimiento regulatorio para programas Golden Visa
- C) Market positioning / branding de oportunidades de inversión paraguayas
- D) Due diligence y certificación de proyectos para inversión extranjera
- E) Network de partners internacionales (abogados de inmigración, family offices, wealth managers)

### B.2 ¿Cuántos proyectos o clientes han asesorado hasta la fecha?

- A) 0-3 (recién comenzando)
- B) 4-10
- C) 11-25
- D) 26-50
- E) 50+

### B.3 ¿Tienen algún caso de éxito o proyecto emblemático que podamos destacar?

- A) Sí, tenemos 1-2 casos concretos que podemos compartir
- B) Sí, tenemos múltiples casos documentados
- C) Tenemos referencias pero no casos públicos
- D) Todavía no tenemos casos para compartir
- E) Preferimos mantener la confidencialidad

---

## C. Audiencia y Contenido

### C.1 ¿Qué tipo de inversionista extranjero busca específicamente Paraguay?

- A) High-net-worth individuals (HNWI, $1M+) buscando segunda residencia
- B) Familias buscando reubicación y educación
- C) Empresarios / dueños de negocio buscando expansión regional
- D) Jubilados con capital para invertir
- E) Inversores institucionales (family offices, fondos)

### C.2 ¿Cuál es el vehículo de inversión típico que estructuran?

- A) Inmobiliario (desarrollos, terrenos, propiedades comerciales)
- B) Deuda privada / bonos
- C) Fondos de inversión / private equity
- D) Agroindustria / commodities
- E) Múltiples vehículos según el proyecto

### C.3 ¿Qué contenido sería más valioso para su audiencia?

- A) Guías paso a paso del proceso de Golden Visa en Paraguay
- B) Análisis de mercado paraguayo para inversión extranjera
- C) Casos de estudio / testimonios de proyectos estructurados
- D) Información regulatoria y legal actualizada
- E) Webinars o contenido descargable (whitepapers, e-books)

---

## D. Competencia y Diferenciación

### D.1 ¿Quiénes son sus principales competidores?

- A) Firmas de abogados locales con práctica de inversión extranjera
- B) Consultoras internacionales con oficina en Paraguay
- C) Agentes Golden Visa globales (Henley & Partners, etc.)
- D) Bancos de inversión / wealth managers locales
- E) No tenemos competencia directa identificada

### D.2 ¿Cuál es su principal ventaja competitiva?

- A) Conocimiento profundo del ecosistema regulatorio paraguayo
- B) Red de contactos con desarrolladores y empresarios locales
- C) Experiencia internacional entendiendo lo que busca un inversor global
- D) Capacidad de estructurar productos innovadores no disponibles en el mercado
- E) Precios más competitivos que alternativas internacionales

---

## E. Presencia Digital y Website

### E.1 ¿Tienen sitio web actualmente?

- A) No, empezamos desde cero
- B) Sí, pero muy básico / desactualizado
- C) Sí, tiene info general pero necesita reforma completa
- D) Sí, funciona bien pero queremos mejorarlo
- E) Solo tenemos LinkedIn

### E.2 ¿Qué idiomas necesita su sitio web?

- A) Solo español
- B) Español + Inglés
- C) Español + Inglés + Portugués
- D) Multilingüe (ES + EN + PT + otros)
- E) Principalmente inglés, español secundario

### E.3 ¿Qué funcionalidades le gustaría tener? (seleccione prioritarias)

- A) Páginas institucionales (nosotros, servicios, equipo, contacto)
- B) Blog / recursos descargables (guías, whitepapers)
- C) Portafolio de proyectos / casos de estudio
- D) Formulario de contacto con clasificación de leads
- E) Calculadora de inversión / retorno estimado

### E.4 ¿Tienen identidad visual definida (logo, colores, tipografía)?

- A) Sí, identidad visual completa
- B) Tenemos logo pero falta paleta y tipografía
- C) Idea general pero nada formalizado
- D) No tenemos nada, necesitamos diseño desde cero
- E) Tenemos elementos pero queremos rediseñar

---

## F. Aspectos Operativos

### F.1 ¿Tienen dominio registrado?

- A) Sí, y está configurado
- B) Sí, pero no en uso
- C) No, necesitamos registrar uno
- D) Tenemos dominio pero queremos cambiarlo
- E) No estoy seguro

### F.2 ¿Quién será el contacto principal para el proyecto?

- A) Yo mismo / el fundador
- B) Un miembro del equipo designado
- C) Un asistente o community manager
- D) Tercero (agencia, diseñador, etc.)
- E) Todavía no definido

### F.3 ¿Hay fecha objetivo para tener el sitio en línea?

- A) Urgente — menos de 2 semanas
- B) Pronto — 2-4 semanas
- C) Normal — 1-2 meses
- D) Sin prisa — más de 2 meses
- E) Solo explorando opciones

---

## G. Pregunta Abierta

**¿Hay algo más que debamos saber sobre su firma, su enfoque, o lo que busca proyectar en este sitio web?**




---
*Gracias por completar el cuestionario. Sus respuestas nos ayudarán a diseñar 
una estrategia digital alineada con su posicionamiento como advisory de inversión internacional.*
"}
